extraction.one_line_summary: DENZ Nico won from a 29-rider breakaway after Bora-Hansgrohe waited until 300m to lead out a sprint he hijacked, while ARMIRAIL Bruno took pink from a chase group 19.5min behind.
tactical_patterns: Ineos controlled early but allowed 29-rider breakaway 12min gap, revealing willingness to surrender pink for stage 14.
Bora-Hansgrohe's BETTIOL launched sprint at 300m from reduced front group, too early for uphill finish allowing DENZ hijack.
ARMIRAIL at 18.5min GC traveled in third group on road, calculating pink takeover while avoiding front chase energy expenditure.

rider_signals.DENZ Nico: positive: second stage win in three days shows emerging sprint opportunism from breakaways on technical finishes
rider_signals.ARMIRAIL Bruno: positive: positioned perfectly in third group to gain pink without burning matches in doomed chase of front four
rider_signals.GEE Derek: neutral: third second-place finish this Giro suggests strong positioning but lacks final 200m acceleration
rider_signals.BAIS David: positive: strategically reclaimed maglia azzurra on Passo Sempione summit before weather worsened, controlling timing of points collection
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: misjudged sprint timing by 100m on uphill finish, leading out competitors from four-man advantage group
